title: [BUG] Import xlsx with macros
author: alanrps
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

I'm having trouble trying to import an .xlsx file with macros. I am working on a general import and when trying to import a spreadsheet with macro, simply the library then in an infinite loop. 

Lib version: ^4.2.0

## Steps To Reproduce

1. Import a spreadsheet containing macros
2. Use a `load` function as below

```javascript
return new Promise((resolve, reject) => workbook.xlsx.load(file)
        .then(readFile => {
        ...
}
```

## The expected behaviour:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/33161962/113968049-b0e6ad00-9808-11eb-88c5-2d4a8147d67c.png)

comments.author: HaaLeo
comments.body: With what release was this issue resolved? @Siemienik

comments.author: Siemienik
comments.body:
It's not fixed yet. I closed all inactive issues to make cleaner point of view over issues.

comments.author: clalexander
comments.body:
I just discovered this issue today.  My code:
```
const workbook = new Workbook();
const buffer = this.getBuffer(body); // body is S3 bucket object body, returns Buffer
await workbook.xlsx.load(buffer);
```
I don't know what it is about the workbook that I'm trying to load that might be causing the infinite loop. The workbook doesn't have any macros.  It has three worksheets and some formulas, but nothing too crazy.

EDIT: I copied just the values of the worksheet I was targeting to a new workbook and it loaded without issue.

comments.author: clalexander
